title: Game of Life: Naive WebGPU implementation
description:
First experiment: Conway's Game of Life with naive WebGPU implementation.

## For Agents
- Create apps/game-of-life/ directory
- Implement CPU-based Game of Life simulation in JavaScript
- Create WebGPU renderer that draws grid to canvas
- Basic UI controls: start/stop, reset, speed
- Use shared WebGPU utilities from core package

## Implementation Steps
1. Set up experiment package structure
2. Create CPU simulation (2D array, naive rules)
3. Initialize WebGPU device and canvas
4. Render grid as colored pixels
5. Add basic UI controls
6. Connect simulation to render loop

## Key Files
- simulation/game-of-life.ts: CPU simulation logic
- rendering/webgpu-renderer.ts: WebGPU rendering
- ui/controls.tsx: React controls
- main.tsx: Experiment entry point

## Testing
- User should verify simulation runs
- Check rendering is correct
- Test controls work
- Verify performance is acceptable for small grids
close_reason: Duplicate of browser_experiments-0tf which was already completed. Game of Life naive implementation is complete with CPU simulation, WebGPU rendering, UI controls, and testing.

title: Create WebGPU core utilities
description:
Build shared WebGPU utilities for device initialization, buffer management, and error handling. These will be used by all experiments.

## For Agents
- Create packages/core/src/webgpu/ directory
- Implement WebGPUContext class for device initialization
- Create buffer/texture creation helpers
- Implement error handling and validation
- Add TypeScript types for WebGPU resources
- No generic utils - each function must have clear purpose

## Key Functions
- createWebGPUDevice(): Initialize adapter and device
- createBuffer(): Helper for common buffer types
- createTexture(): Helper for 2D textures
- compileShader(): WGSL shader compilation
- WebGPUError: Custom error class for WebGPU errors

## Testing
- User should test device creation works
- Verify error messages are helpful
- Check TypeScript types are correct
close_reason: WebGPU core utilities implemented with device initialization, buffer/texture helpers, error handling, and comprehensive tests. All checks pass.

title: Set up project monorepo structure
description:
Create monorepo structure with pnpm workspaces, TypeScript, Vite, and basic configuration. This is the foundation for all experiments.

## For Agents
- Use pnpm workspaces for package management
- Root package.json with workspaces: ['packages/*', 'apps/*']
- TypeScript base config with strict mode
- Vite for building and development
- ESLint/Prettier for code quality
- No utils directories - everything must have a clear purpose

## Structure


## Testing
- User should verify structure and build works
- Run  ERR_PNPM_NO_PKG_MANIFEST  No package.json found in /Users/iangeorge/Code/browser_experiments and  ERR_PNPM_NO_IMPORTER_MANIFEST_FOUND  No package.json (or package.yaml, or package.json5) was found in "/Users/iangeorge/Code/browser_experiments". to test
close_reason: Monorepo structure successfully set up with pnpm workspaces, TypeScript, Vite, ESLint, Prettier, and Vitest. All checks pass.

title: Refine Game of Life drawing tools UX and fix tiling issues
description:
Multiple UX issues with drawing tools implementation:

1. **Tiling bug**: Drawing tools only work in top-left copy when grid tiles
2. **Spacing issues**: Tool buttons have poor spacing/layout
3. **Random density**: Clicking random changes the density slider value
4. **Simplify tools**: Remove invert, rotate, flip H/V (too complex)
5. **Save/load UX**: Replace text input with filename dialog, add load function
6. **Pattern placement**: Built-in patterns should be placed like Photoshop brushes (not clear+center)
7. **Toolbar design**: Make it look more like Photoshop toolbar
8. **Future**: Load images and convert to GoL patterns (separate bead)

Need to fix tiling math, improve UI layout, simplify tools, and enhance pattern placement.

title: Fix inverted Y-axis in Game of Life canvas clicking
description: Canvas click-to-toggle has inverted Y-axis: clicking at top of canvas affects bottom row, clicking at bottom affects top row. Root cause: texture coordinates have (0,0) at bottom-left, but grid data is stored with row 0 at top. Fixed by flipping texture data vertically in updateGrid method.
close_reason: Fixed by flipping texture data vertically in updateGrid method. Texture coordinates have (0,0) at bottom-left, but grid data is stored with row 0 at top. Now canvas clicking works correctly: top of canvas = top row, bottom of canvas = bottom row.

title: Fix GitHub Actions test workflow pnpm version conflict
description: GitHub Actions test workflow is failing due to pnpm version conflict. The workflow specifies 'version: 10' but package.json has 'packageManager: pnpm@10.6.5'. Need to remove the version from workflow to avoid conflict. Also fixed linting/typecheck issues: removed unused handleRandomize function, changed console.log to console.warn, fixed any types in performance monitor.
close_reason: Fixed pnpm version conflict in GitHub Actions test workflow. Removed version: 10 from workflow to avoid conflict with package.json's packageManager: pnpm@10.6.5. Also fixed associated linting/typecheck issues.

title: Create visual debugging skill for WebGPU/canvas applications
description:
Create a skill that enables LLM agents to capture and analyze visual output from browser graphics applications (WebGPU, Canvas, WebGL).

## Purpose
Enable effective debugging of rendering issues by providing agents with 'eyes' into the browser. Skill captures screenshots, canvas pixel data, and WebGPU state for analysis.

## Skill Components
1. **Skill definition** (SKILL.md): Usage patterns, prerequisites, examples
2. **Playwright scripts**:
   - : Canvas pixel data as JSON
   - : Full page screenshots
   - : WebGPU context inspection
   - : Visual diff generation
3. **Data processing utilities**:
   - Image to Base64 conversion
   - Pixel data analysis and summarization
   - JSON serialization of rendering state
4. **Integration hooks**:
   - File naming conventions ()
   - Agent workflow integration
   - Error handling and fallbacks

## Technical Requirements
1. **Playwright setup**:
   - Install , , 
   - Configure WebGPU browser flags ()
   - Handle headless/headed mode for WebGPU support
2. **Dev server coordination**:
   - Check if Vite server running, start if needed
   - Alternative: Use built site ()
3. **Output handling**:
   - Predictable file locations for agent access
   - Structured JSON with metadata (settings, errors, state)
   - Optional Base64 output for chat transmission

## Use Cases
1. **Game of Life debugging**: Capture canvas when rendering issues occur
2. **Visual regression**: Compare against baseline renders
3. **WebGPU state inspection**: Debug initialization failures
4. **Performance analysis**: Capture frame timing, memory usage

## For Agents
- Skill provides 'eyes' for debugging rendering issues
- Focus on Game of Life use case first
- Output should be agent-accessible (files in )
- Include simulation state with captures for context
close_reason: Visual debugging skill created and tested successfully. Skill captures screenshots of WebGPU applications, provides multi-LLM compatibility (file paths + Base64), and has been used to debug Game of Life UI issues. Located at ~/.config/opencode/skills/visual-debugging/

title: Fix Game of Life core bugs and simplify UI
description:
Fix critical bugs in Game of Life experiment and simplify the user interface.

## Current Issues
1. **Broken controls**: Only start/pause/step/reset work (should be stop/step/run/reset)
2. **Grid configuration non-functional**: Grid size, cell size, speed controls don't work properly
3. **Patterns broken**: Glider/blinker add to wrong positions, not visible
4. **Color themes broken**: Cells always green regardless of theme selection
5. **CSS build errors**: Preventing deployment
6. **Initialization issues**: Effect uses stale state values

## Fixes Required
1. **Simplify controls**: Stop/Step/Run/Reset only (remove Pause, rename Start→Run)
2. **Fix grid configuration**:
   - Grid size: Recreate simulation when changed
   - Cell size: Update renderer properly  
   - Speed: Control simulation FPS correctly
3. **Remove broken features**:
   - Remove color themes (use #F0F0F0 for alive, #000000 for dead)
   - Remove pattern buttons (add back in future issue)
4. **Fix initialization**: Update effect dependencies to use current state
5. **Fix CSS**: Resolve build errors

## For Agents
- Focus on making core simulation work correctly
- Remove complexity before adding features back
- Test each control independently
- Verify rendering works with simple black/white scheme
close_reason: Fixed all core bugs: CSS build error, simplified controls (Run/Stop/Step/Reset), fixed grid configuration, removed broken color themes and pattern buttons, fixed initialization dependencies. All controls tested and working.

title: Optimize Game of Life with compute shaders
description:
Optimize the Game of Life experiment by moving simulation from CPU to GPU using WebGPU compute shaders, within the unified showcase static site.

## Current Status (Naive Implementation in Unified Architecture)
- CPU-based simulation in apps/showcase/src/experiments/game-of-life/
- Full texture updates every frame
- Performance limited by JavaScript execution
- Separate simulation and rendering steps
- Part of single static site build

## Optimization Goals (Within Unified Architecture)
1. Move simulation to GPU: Use compute shaders for parallel cell updates within existing experiment directory
2. Implement double buffering: Ping-pong storage textures for simulation
3. Reduce memory transfers: Keep data on GPU between frames
4. Optimize workgroup sizes: Match GPU architecture for best performance
5. Add performance comparison: Show CPU vs GPU performance metrics

## Implementation Tasks (Unified Architecture)
1. Create compute shader for Game of Life rules in existing experiment directory
2. Implement double buffering with storage textures
3. Update existing renderer to read from simulation texture
4. Add performance comparison visualization
5. Create benchmarking mode for optimization validation
6. Ensure single build process still works: pnpm build

## For Agents
- Work within existing apps/showcase/src/experiments/game-of-life/ directory
- Study existing WebGPU compute shader patterns
- Focus on memory access patterns optimization
- Implement proper error handling for compute shaders
- Document performance improvements within experiment
- Include fallback to CPU simulation if compute shaders fail
- Maintain single site build integrity

## Testing
- User should verify simulation correctness matches CPU version at /experiments/game-of-life
- Test performance improvement metrics
- Check memory usage comparison
- Verify compute shader fallback works
- Test on different GPU architectures if possible
- Ensure site still builds as single unit

title: Optimize visual debugging skill with sub-agent architecture
description:
The current visual debugging skill outputs Base64 image data directly in JSON, consuming significant context tokens. Create a sub-agent architecture where:

1. Main skill captures screenshots and saves to files only (no Base64 in output)
2. New 'visual-analyzer' sub-agent can be invoked to analyze screenshots
3. Sub-agent receives file paths and uses vision capabilities to answer questions
4. Optional: Add image analysis features (detect rendering issues, count cells, etc.)
5. Maintain multi-LLM compatibility: file paths for file-access agents, sub-agent for vision models

Benefits:
- Reduces context token usage by ~100KB+ per capture
- Enables targeted visual analysis without polluting main context
- More scalable for complex debugging sessions
- Can add specialized analysis features over time
notes: Visual debugging sub-agent architecture implemented. Created visual-analyzer agent with GPT-5 nano, updated capture skill with --save-to-project option, created integration helper script. Ready for UI bug fixing.
